role_title: Associate Manager, Financial Products - Strategy & Operations

level_signals: Comp range $105,400–$155,000 base consistent with mid-level IC

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: You have 4+ years of experience in strategy & operations / product management / investment banking / management consulting / mergers & acquisitions or related experience (preferable in the financing or fin tech industry)
You're energized by diving into difficult problems with first principles and working cross-functionally with multi-disciplinary teams.
You're a logic & data-driven problem solver that can leverage Excel/Google Sheets to derive answers (SQL experience is a plus)
Experience working with data analytics and predictive modeling
You want to affect change every day and can operate in energizing environments

nice_to_haves.raw_text_from_jd: preferable in the financing or fin tech industry

scope.surface: Merchant financial products (financing space); early-stage growth product with a key external partner
scope.business_model: B2B platform (merchant-facing financial products within DoorDash marketplace)
primary_metrics_inferred: Growth of financing product from early stage to maturity
core_responsibilities: Understand the needs of our Merchants, prioritize those needs by impact, and create solutions with cross-functional partners
Drive the implementation of new product features and processes for managing Merchant financial products
Navigate in an ambiguous environment and bring structure to undefined opportunities
Leverage data whenever possible to inform your hypothesis and convince cross functional partners
Work cross-functionally with our Product, Engineering, Sales, Marketing, Finance, and Business Development teams to align on a path forward and bring these products to market

green_flags: Equity grants mentioned as part of compensation
Clear reporting structure (reports to General Manager of Merchant Financials)
Early-stage product ownership with room to define playbooks — high growth potential
Strong cross-functional scope across Product, Engineering, Sales, Marketing, Finance, and BD
red_flags: Strictly onsite at SF HQ — no remote or hybrid option stated, limiting candidate pool
Role is described as early/undefined product ('no playbooks or templates') which may signal lack of organizational support
Visa sponsorship not addressed in the JD

fields_inferred_not_stated: seniority.level (inferred from title 'Associate Manager', years required, and comp range)
scope.business_model (inferred from product description and team context)
scope.primary_metrics_inferred (inferred from responsibilities; not explicitly stated)
location.office_time_required (inferred from 'The candidate will be expected to be in office (SF HQ)')
compensation.location_pay_differential (inferred from 'Base salary is localized according to an employee's work location')
ambiguities: The JD lists 4+ years across a wide range of functions (strategy & ops, PM, IB, consulting, M&A) with no clear hierarchy of preference among them — treated as a single compound hard requirement.

The 'preferable in the financing or fin tech industry' language is attached to the years-of-experience sentence, making it ambiguous whether it is a separate soft requirement or a modifier on the hard one — extracted separately as a nice-to-have for queryability.
The national pay range is described as applying to 'Illinois and Colorado' as well, but SF is the required location; pay differential logic may apply.
